name: Vegetarian Tamales
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the dried corn husks in cold water for 1 hour.
2. For the filling, soak the TVP® in 7/8 cup hot water for 5 minutes.
3. In a skillet, sauté olive oil, onion, garlic, and green pepper.
4. Add chili powder, cumin, garlic salt, tomato puree, and Cayenne to the reconstituted TVP®.
5. Mix TVP® and onions together and heat for 2 minutes. Taste the filling and add a little hot sauce if desired.
6. To make the masa dough, combine masa harina and salt in a bowl. Slowly mix in warm water (about 1 1/2 cups) until it forms a firm but moist dough. Work in margarine or oil while kneading.
7. Flatten the masa dough into patties that are quarter-inch thick using your hand.
8. Place a spoonful of filling in the center of each patty and fold over to enclose the filling, wrapping tightly with corn husk pieces.
9. If the husks are small or broken, use multiple pieces.
10. Fill a kettle with 1 cup hot water, place a steamer rack inside, and arrange the tamales on the rack. Cover the pan and bring the water to a boil. Reduce heat and steam the tamales for 45 minutes. Cooking in batches may be necessary depending on the size of your steamer.
11. Makes 36-40 tamales.
Per tamale: 44 calories; 2 grams protein; 6 grams carbohydrate; 2 grams fat
